title: NQ Gap Fill Balance Reclaim
focus_questions: Did the market only touch the gap, or did it actually accept back into old value?
Is the gap still the active script anchor after the first fill attempt?

summary.thesis: A meaningful U.S. session gap becomes actionable when the repair path back into old value is supported by post-fill acceptance rather than a one-touch fill.
summary.operator_translation: Trade the reaction after the fill or deep partial fill, not the headline that a gap exists.
summary.ai_usage_note: Prefer this card when the replay contains opening-gap context, fill progression, and post-fill acceptance or rejection evidence.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: U.S. session gap acting as the opening script anchor. Required evidence: first touch or fill progression plus clear post-fill acceptance or rejection. Do not trade: just because a gap exists or because price touched the fill level once. Real trigger: accepted return into old value or confirmed rejection after partial or full fill. Failure point: the gap stops mattering or post-fill behavior stays noisy and contradictory.

when_relevant: The session opens with a meaningful RTH gap relative to prior close or value.
Price begins repairing back toward the gap in an organized way.
The operator needs to know whether the fill becomes balance reclaim or rejection.
required_evidence: gap_opening_context remains active in the replay window
first touch, partial fill, and full fill are distinguishable
post-fill acceptance or rejection is observable after the fill attempt
confirmation_signals: approach quality into the gap is organized rather than random drift only
trade begins holding inside old value after the fill or deep partial fill
follow-through confirms that the gap acted as a path back into balance rather than a one-touch magnet
invalidation_signals: the gap stops acting as the dominant script anchor
post-fill acceptance never appears after the fill attempt
the market fills and immediately rejects without any balance reclaim
no_trade_conditions: Do not trade just because a gap exists; the gap must still be the active script anchor.
Do not fade or follow the fill blindly if post-fill acceptance is not visible yet.
Do not treat overnight noise and U.S. regular-session gap behavior as the same thing without session context.

entry_archetypes.name: accepted return into old value
entry_archetypes.description: Trade after the fill or deep partial fill is accepted back into prior value.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if price only tags old value and rejects immediately.

entry_archetypes.name: partial-fill rejection
entry_archetypes.description: Trade the rejection only when the partial fill clearly fails and the open script remains dominant.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the fill depth is shallow and still likely to continue repairing.

management_notes: Keep fill depth and post-fill acceptance separate in analysis.
If price accepts inside old value, shift the target logic toward balance rotation.
If price rejects after the fill, the script is no longer balance reclaim and should be relabeled.
review_questions: Was the operator trading the gap itself, or the post-fill acceptance or rejection?
Did price truly accept back into old value after the fill, or only tag it briefly?
Was the opening context strong enough to keep the gap as the main script anchor?
